summary: Large IF-module decompilation PR focused on soundtest plus related if_2F72, ifmagnify, ifstatus, types, and header/prototype updates. The bot report records +5388 matched bytes, 57 new 100% matches, and 31 partial-function improvements. Reviewer approved while noting that some code was rough and contained many unrolled loops.

key_files.role: Largest changed source file; soundtest decompilation/matching work with many newly matched or improved functions reported by bot.
evidence_refs: changed_files: soundtest.c added 843 deleted 67 hunks 17
text_corpus: bot report lists many main/melee/if/soundtest new matches and improvements

key_files.role: Header paired with soundtest.c; significant prototype/header churn.
evidence_refs: changed_files: soundtest.h added 63 deleted 63 hunks 3

key_files.role: Adds many IF animation/object/audio helper implementations and replaces a literal archive section argument with a static string symbol.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: static char lbl_803F9780[] = "ScInfStc_scene_models" and lbArchive_LoadSections now uses lbl_803F9780
text_corpus: bot report lists several if_2F72 100% matches and partial improvements

key_files.role: Updates prototypes from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to typed signatures and adds if/forward.h include.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: fn_802F7288 becomes s32 fn_802F7288(HSD_GObj*, Element_803F9628*)
raw_diff: fn_802F75D4/fn_802F7670/fn_802F770C/fn_802F77F8/fn_802F7994 typed

key_files.role: Adds magnify camera/resource setup and state helper implementations.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: implements ifMagnify_802FC618, ifMagnify_802FC750, ifMagnify_802FC7C0, ifMagnify_802FC870, ifMagnify_802FC8E8, ifMagnify_802FC940
text_corpus: bot report lists ifMagnify_802FC618 and ifMagnify_802FC7C0 as 100% matches and other ifmagnify improvements

key_files.role: Updates ifmagnify prototypes and adds extern string declaration.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS entries changed to typed void signatures
raw_diff: adds extern char ifMagnify_804D57E8[]

key_files.role: Changes ifStatus_803F9628 from static to externally visible and adjusts Element_803F9628 initializers.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: static Element_803F9628 ifStatus_803F9628[8] becomes Element_803F9628 ifStatus_803F9628[8]
changed_files: ifstatus.c added 9 deleted 9

key_files.role: Header support for ifstatus data/functions used by if_2F72.
evidence_refs: changed_files: ifstatus.h added 4 deleted 0 hunks 2
raw_diff: if_2F72.c includes if/ifstatus.h and references ifStatus_803F9628 and ifStatus_802F4910

key_files.role: Type support for recovered IF structures such as Element_803F9628 and ifMagnify fields.
evidence_refs: changed_files: types.h added 20 deleted 4 hunks 1
raw_diff: if_2F72.c uses Element_803F9628 typed fields and ifmagnify.c uses ifMagnify

key_files.role: Small baselib header addition associated with this PR.
evidence_refs: changed_files: sobjlib.h added 1 deleted 0 hunks 1

what_changed: Added implementations for a block of src/melee/if/if_2F72.c functions that create, animate, update, and destroy HSD_GObj/HSD_JObj-based interface objects and trigger audio callbacks.
Added several src/melee/if/ifmagnify.c implementations for magnify camera setup, cleanup, color/default initialization, archive loading, and ignore_offscreen state toggles.
Updated if_2F72.h and ifmagnify.h prototypes from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to typed signatures for recovered bodies.
Exposed ifStatus_803F9628 outside ifstatus.c and adjusted its initializer shape to match the Element_803F9628 type used by if_2F72.c.
Touched soundtest.c and soundtest.h heavily; the loaded diff is truncated, but bot output attributes many new soundtest matches and improvements to this PR.
Added or adjusted include dependencies across IF files and one baselib header.
smart_moves: Paired body recovery with header prototype cleanup rather than leaving recovered functions behind UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS.
Introduced typed access to Element_803F9628 and ifMagnify data in several places instead of keeping every access fully opaque.
Used narrow subsystem includes for newly referenced systems such as gm, lb, player, ground, and baselib object/camera APIs.
Bot report documents measurable text-match progress, including 57 new 100% matches and 31 additional improvements.

decomp_lessons.lesson: When recovering a batch of related interface functions, update the owning headers as signatures become known so callsites can use typed prototypes.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: if_2F72.h changes multiple UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS declarations to typed signatures
raw_diff: ifmagnify.h changes ifMagnify_802FC3BC through ifMagnify_802FC940 declarations to typed signatures

decomp_lessons.lesson: Large match-progress PRs can still leave reviewability debt; reviewer explicitly flagged unrolled loops as rough despite approving the match progress.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: ribbanya review says "Some of this is pretty rough, for example there are a lot of unrolled loops. But I can't argue with the match progress."
raw_diff: if_2F72.c shows repeated slot-search ladders in fn_802F770C/fn_802F7994 and explicit repeated player assignments in ifMagnify_802FC8E8/ifMagnify_802FC940

decomp_lessons.lesson: Bot match reports are useful for intake evidence of match progress, but the PR slice does not include local build commands, objdiff screenshots, or author reasoning for specific rough shapes.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: decomp-dev bot report lists matched-code percentage and new matches
loaded_files: no inline review comments and no author validation details beyond PR body

naming_conventions.convention: Unrecovered or weakly understood functions remain address-style names such as fn_802F7288, if_802F73C4, and un_8030178C while signatures are improved.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: if_2F72.c/h keep fn_ and if_ address-style names
text_corpus: bot report lists many soundtest un_ and fn_ address-style symbols

naming_conventions.convention: Local variables use short role names where roles are evident, such as gobj, jobj, cobj, entry, base, slot, idx, frame, and viewport.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: if_2F72.c functions use HSD_GObj* gobj, HSD_JObj* jobj, Element_803F9628* entry, s32 slot
raw_diff: ifmagnify.c uses HSD_CObj* cobj, HSD_ImageDesc* idesc, HSD_RectS16 viewport

naming_conventions.convention: Some data symbols remain address-style labels such as lbl_803F9780 and lbl_804A1340 when semantic ownership is not fully recovered in the slice.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: static char lbl_803F9780[] = "ScInfStc_scene_models"
raw_diff: static void* lbl_804A1340[13]

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use explicit typed prototypes and forward declarations to make recovered functions callable across IF files.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: if_2F72.h adds if/forward.h and Element_803F9628 parameter
raw_diff: ifmagnify.h adds typed ifMagnify* parameter for ifMagnify_802FC7C0

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Retain some generated-looking or unrolled slot logic where it contributed to match progress, but mark it as cleanup risk because reviewer called out many unrolled loops.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: approved review notes rough code and many unrolled loops
raw_diff: repeated slot comparisons in fn_802F770C and fn_802F7994

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: A volatile local was used for a default color value in ifMagnify_802FC7C0, likely as a narrow codegen steering tactic; no verifier rationale is present in the slice.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ifMagnify_802FC7C0 declares volatile int default_val = ifMagnify_804DDB60
decomp_standards: matching-tactics-need-evidence requires explicit verifier evidence for volatile locals

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Raw byte-offset access appears in places where struct fields were not fully modeled, e.g. ((u8*) status)[slot * 100 + 16] and player0 pointer arithmetic.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: fn_802F770C checks ((u8*) status)[slot * 100 + 16]
raw_diff: ifMagnify_802FC618 uses u8* player0 = (u8*) &ifMagnify_804A1DE0 + 0x14

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Expanded jobj assertions and manual dirty-flag logic were kept in fn_802F77F8 rather than mapped to a higher-level inline in the visible diff.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: fn_802F77F8 contains __assert("jobj.h", 0x394, "jobj"), __assert("jobj.h", 0x395, "jobj"), and __assert("jobj.h", 0x234, "jobj")
decomp_standards: header-inlines recommends mapping expanded jobj.h asserts back to existing HSD_JObj helpers when evidence identifies them

evidence_refs: text_corpus: review by ribbanya at 2026-01-09T11:15:17Z

follow_up_queries: PR2075 unrolled loops if_2F72 fn_802F770C fn_802F7994
PR2075 ifMagnify_802FC8E8 ifMagnify_802FC940 repeated player ignore_offscreen

notes: The soundtest.c diff is not visible in loaded excerpts despite being the largest changed file, so soundtest-specific code lessons are limited to changed-file metadata and bot report symbols.
No inline review comments are present.
The bot report supports match-progress claims, but the slice does not include author-run build commands, objdiff screenshots, or rationale for individual codegen tactics.
Some observations about volatile locals, expanded asserts, and raw byte offsets are framed as tactics or follow-up risks, not accepted standards or reviewer intent.

accepted_candidate_records.title: PR2075: match progress may land with known unrolled-loop debt
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR2075, a large IF-module matching batch was approved after bot-reported progress of +5388 matched bytes, 57 new matches, and 31 improvements, but reviewer ribbanya explicitly noted that some code was rough and had many unrolled loops. Use this as historical evidence that match-progress PRs can carry cleanup debt rather than as approval of unrolled loops as a style.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: decomp-dev bot report for GALE01 shows +5388 matched bytes
text_corpus: ribbanya approved while noting rough code and many unrolled loops

accepted_candidate_records.title: PR2075: body recovery was accompanied by header prototype cleanup
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR2075 updated if_2F72.h and ifmagnify.h from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to typed prototypes for functions whose bodies were added in the same PR. This is useful historical evidence for keeping headers truthful when recovering IF-module functions.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ifmagnify.h typed ifMagnify_802FC3BC through ifMagnify_802FC940

source_update_candidates.title: Use PR2075 as an example for natural-loop cleanup debt, not as precedent for preserving unrolled loops
source_update_candidates.text: PR2075 contains reviewer-approved match progress but the approval comment explicitly calls out many unrolled loops as rough. Curator may want to cite this PR under natural-loop or reviewability guidance as evidence that unrolled loops can be known debt even when a match-progress PR lands.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: ribbanya review says there are a lot of unrolled loops
text_corpus: bot report shows substantial match progress
source_update_candidates.reason: The loaded standards already prefer natural loops; this PR provides historical review evidence that unrolled code was considered rough, not stylistically preferred.

source_update_candidates.title: PR2075 examples for tactics needing evidence: volatile local, expanded jobj asserts, and raw offset access
source_update_candidates.text: The visible diff includes a volatile default_val in ifMagnify_802FC7C0, expanded jobj.h asserts/manual dirty-flag handling in fn_802F77F8, and raw byte-offset access in IF status/magnify code. Curator may use these as searchable examples of codegen tactics or cleanup targets that require verifier evidence rather than as accepted style.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: volatile int default_val = ifMagnify_804DDB60
raw_diff: fn_802F77F8 expanded __assert("jobj.h", ...) blocks
raw_diff: ((u8*) status)[slot * 100 + 16] and player0 byte-offset access
source_update_candidates.reason: The PR evidence shows these patterns but does not provide enough validation rationale to accept them as standards.

rejection_notes: Do not infer that all functions in soundtest.c are cleanly matched from source diff evidence; the bot report lists matches, but the loaded soundtest diff is absent/truncated.
Do not promote the use of volatile locals, expanded header asserts, raw byte offsets, or unrolled loops as accepted style from this PR.
Do not infer reviewer intent beyond the explicit approval comment: reviewer accepted match progress while calling out rough unrolled code.
